name: Candied Roots, Fruits, or Flowers
recipeIngredient: 2 large Carrots or parsnips, scraped and sliced into 1/8‑inch discs
2 small Apples or pears, peeled, cored, and cut into 1/8‑inch slices
1 cup Fresh flower petals (trim off white tips which attach petals to stem)
recipeInstructions:
To candy any Roots, Fruits, or Flowers—Dissolve sugar (or sugar‑candy) in rose water. Bring the mixture to a boil. Add the vegetable, fruit, or flower pieces while the syrup is cold. Allow it to rest for a moment; then remove them and bring the syrup back to a boil. Return the pieces to the syrup and continue boiling until the desired hardness is reached (adding more sugar but not rose water). Remove the items from the syrup when it is cool and let them stand until candy‑hard before peeling off the candies onto waxed paper. Store in an airtight container.
Yield: about 2 dozen candies
